message:
bd-1xma.5: add criterion benchmarks for E-graph vs greedy crossing minimization

Benchmark suite comparing E-graph equality saturation against greedy
barycenter/sifting heuristics for crossing minimization:

Benchmark groups:
- crossing_min/sparse_dag: 10/20/50 nodes per layer, 5 layers, 10% density
- crossing_min/dense_dag: 5/10/15/20 nodes per layer, 3 layers, 40% density
- crossing_min/bipartite: K_{5,5}, K_{10,10}, K_{5,15} complete bipartite
- crossing_min/quality_summary: comparison metrics across all graph types

Test graph generators:
- generate_layered_graph(): parameterized DAG with configurable density
- generate_bipartite(): complete bipartite graphs K_{n,m}

Metrics collected:
- Crossing count (C_e vs C_g)
- Runtime (T_e vs T_g)
- Improvement ratio: (C_g - C_e) / C_g * 100%
- Strategy used (egraph_completed, greedy_won, etc.)

4 unit tests verify generators and comparison logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -40,3 +40,7 @@ criterion = { version = "0.5", features = ["html_reports"] }
 [[bench]]
 name = "incremental_layout"
 harness = false
+
+[[bench]]
+name = "crossing_minimization"
+harness = false
